title: Evangelions soundtrack
wikitext:
[]: Evangelions soundtrack             Quote Post by 93rd » Tue Apr 21, 2020 8:40 pm If Evangelions soundtrack had not been composed by Shirō Sagisu, how different would the shows mood be? If it had somehow found its way into the hands of someone like Yoko Kanno, would the soundtrack be as loved as it is today? I'm sure I'm not the only person who thinks that the soundtrack, to really any form of media, is one of the most important aspects of it. I think that if Neon Genesis Evangelion's soundtrack had not been as strong as it was, it would've affected the shows reception, maybe negatively, maybe not.

[]: Re: Evangelions soundtrack             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Apr 22, 2020 8:36 am That's a question entirely without answer. There are a lot of composers that would probably have produced reults with a similar feel; at the same time, there are countless others that would have produced a soundtrack that felt differently in just about as many ways.

[]: Re: Evangelions soundtrack             Quote Post by 93rd » Wed Apr 22, 2020 2:00 pm Blockio wrote: That's a question entirely without answer.

[]: Re: Evangelions soundtrack             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Apr 22, 2020 5:32 pm The best composers produce music that varies widely according to the requirements of the show concerned. Sagisu or Kanno, for instance, can write in a wide variety of styles (Kanno more than Sagisu, I'd say overall ). So it could be that the music in Evangelion is to a considerable extent the right music for that show and would have turned out that way whichever (decent) composer had been asked to write it.

[]: Re: Evangelions soundtrack             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Apr 23, 2020 5:39 am I'd say there are certain "Sagisuisms" though which are recurring in his work which I would say perfectly fit Eva. Can't properly explain cause I don't know music that well. It's the way he ends certain themes, there's just something deeply melancholic about it. Kanno is amazing too though.

[]: Re: Evangelions soundtrack             Quote Post by 93rd » Fri Apr 24, 2020 8:07 pm pwhodges wrote: So it could be that the music in Evangelion is to a considerable extent the right music for that show and would have turned out that way whichever (decent) composer had been asked to write it.
